title: 血液塑料回收计划 Re - plastic Blood plan
desc: 这是一个关于过滤血液中的微塑料并回收的思辨设计。如果要选择一种材料代表人类世，塑料当之无愧。当下，塑料被大量生产、使用与废弃后却没有得到合理的处理。废弃塑料经过自然环境的洗礼，产生了大量微塑料，融入了生态圈。在对生物圈内富集。前不久，我关注到了荷兰科学家研究中发现了人体血液中存在微塑料。这个新闻引发我的深思，这种情况是否最终会对人体造成伤害？我们是否有可能把微塑料从血液中回收出来？不断上升的塑料产量和不断在自然界富集的微塑料是否会改变现有的社会关系？为此，我设计了未来人类处理人体内富集的微塑料的产品。在未来，人体中的塑料含量不断增大，人类不得不进行日常的血液塑料回收，但环境中已经大量存在的微塑料以及会源源不断生产的塑料都会持续加重环境负担产生进一步的生态危机。为此，我设想了“血液塑料回收计划”：政府全面禁止新生塑料的生产，所有的塑料都由人体血液回收塑料而来，由政府监管的工厂才能生产塑料制品，个人血液回收塑料通过政府提供的平台兑换成“塑料积分” ，通过“塑料积分” 在平台上兑换必要的塑料制品。在“血液塑料回收计划”的作用下，人体血液塑料、环境微塑料、塑料制品三者达成了一种循环，世界塑料总量不变。这种人造制品最终还是通过人在环境和人类社会之间达到了平衡。
desc_en: A thought-provoking design for filtering microplastics from blood and recycling them. If we had to choose one material to represent the Anthropocene, plastic would deserve it. Nowadays, plastics are mass-produced, used and discarded without reasonable treatment. Waste plastic through the natural environment baptism, produced a large number of microplastics, into the ecosphere. In to the biosphere within the enrichment. Not long ago, I paid attention to the Dutch scientists in the study found the existence of microplastics in human blood. This news triggered me to think deeply about whether this situation will eventually cause harm to human beings. Is it possible for us to recycle microplastics out of blood? Will the rising production of plastics and the constant enrichment of microplastics in nature change the existing social relations? For this reason, I have designed products for future humans to deal with microplastics enriched in the human body. In the future, the increasing amount of plastic in the human body will force humans to recycle blood plastics on a daily basis, but microplastics that are already present in large quantities in the environment as well as plastics that will be produced continuously will continue to burden the environment and create further ecological crises. In this regard, I envisioned the "Blood Plastic Recycling Programme": the government completely banned the production of newborn plastics, all plastics come from human blood recycled plastics, and only government-regulated factories can produce plastics, and personal blood recycled plastics can be exchanged for "Plastic Points" through the platform provided by the government. Individual blood plastics are converted into "Plastic Points" through a platform provided by the government, through which the Plastic Points are redeemed for necessary plastic products. Under the "Blood Plastic Recycling Programme", human blood plastics, environmental microplastics and plastic products have reached a cycle, and the total amount of plastic in the world remains unchanged. This man-made product ultimately strikes a balance between the environment and human society through human beings.
honor:
2024年, Fly Me to the Moon - Tongji University D&I | Dutch Design Week

2024年，米兰 作品入选2024米兰设计周卫星沙龙同济展区, Blood Plastic Recycling Plan | Fly Me To The Moon
thanks: 感谢父母对于这项工作提供的资助。

title: LINKIO-链界
desc: 该界面充当数字空间与物理空间之间的桥梁，通过数字领域连接遥远的地点。它探索了有形的交互方式，为工程师和艺术家提供了开源工具。凭借多功能支持和人工智能驱动的交互模式，它增强了人机连接。该项目从团队合作的角度探索了空间中有形界面的交互潜力、有形界面与 AIGC 结合的潜力以及数字物质化。
desc_en: The Interface serves as a bridge between digital and physical spaces, connecting distant locations through the digital realm. It explores tangible interactions, offering open-source tools for engineers and artists. With multifunctional support and AI-driven interaction modes, it enhances human-machine connections.This project explores the interaction potential of tangible interface in space, the potential of combining tangible interface with AIGC, and digital materialization from the perspective of group cooperation.
honor:
1.《LINKIO—迈向空间的有形交互》意大利米兰设计周沙龙展参展

2.《Linkio》KPA鲲鹏奖数字设计优秀奖

3.中国设计智造大奖 佳作奖

title: X-brain
tags: China International College Students' Innovation Competition
desc: 作为全球领先的中风治疗技术领导者，X-Brain 一直在不断扩大中风治疗的时间窗口。基于对蝶腭神经节刺激对脑血流影响的前沿医学研究，X-Brain 通过微针植入为脑梗死患者提供周期性、高效、安全且可控的电刺激，能够有效治疗超过 8 小时时间窗范围内的中风患者。凭借 1000 亿的市场规模作为支撑，X-Brain 利用成本低、耗材少和周期短的技术优势填补了治疗空白，为数以千万计的患者带来了新生。X-Brain 致力于打造全球领先的中风治疗方案，坚守“拯救数百万‘无法治愈’的中风患者”的企业使命，以科技的力量改善人类福祉。
desc_en: As the global leader in stroke treatment technology, X-Brain has opened the maximum time window of stroke treatment wider and wider. Based on the cutting-edge medical research on the effect of sphenopalate ganglion stimulation on cerebral blood flow, X-Brain provides periodic, efficient, safe and controllable electrical stimulation for patients with cerebral infarction through microneedle implantation, which can effectively treat stroke patients outside the window of 8 hours. Backed by the market scale of 100 billion, X-Brain fills the treatment gap with the technical advantages of low cost, low consumable and short cycle, and gives tens of millions of patients a new life. X-Brain is committed to building the world's leading stroke treatment program, adhering to the corporate mission of saving tens of millions of `incurable` stroke patients, and improving human well-being with the power of technology.
honor:
2023 年中国国际大学生创新竞赛 金奖 新医科组第一名

China International College Students' Innovation Competition 2023

Gold Award The first place in the new medical department

title: 偕童行者——移动可穿戴微型机器人
desc: POPA 是一款专为儿童设计的移动可穿戴微型机器人。基于儿童身体自我概念的探索，其能够以智能陪伴宠物的形式随身携带且在衣物上自由爬行。通过多种与之互动的游戏模式，引导儿童参与独特且富有趣味性的运动方式与交互体验，让他们在玩乐中无意识地锻炼与探索身体，提升身体协调性，同时也培养他们的创新思维与团队协作能力。
desc_en: POPA is a mobile wearable micro robot specifically designed for children. Based on the exploration of children's self-concept of the body, it can be carried around as an intelligent companion pet and freely crawl on clothes. Through various interactive game modes, it guides children to participate in unique and interesting movement methods and interactive experiences, allowing them to unconsciously exercise and explore their bodies, improve body coordination, and also cultivate their innovative thinking and teamwork skills.
honor: 2024年中国设计智造大奖 佳作奖

title: 玩吧！｜2023 YOUNG to young国际联合设计课程PLAYSHOP主题片
desc: PLAYSHOP 2023的主视觉以「群岛」为命题:当技术的海平面上升，旧的山峰与高原变为一座座海上浮堡，众峰不再独绝。在温暖洋流的滋养下，每个岛屿衍生出其独有形态，群岛间亦滋生出新的生态，彼此互联互望，新的对话由此打开。
thanks: 冯宇纯、王志鹏、诸葛瑞静

title: 颅内花园 `Former Garden
desc: 这部短片是一次深入内心深处的自我探索之旅，它将观众带入个人心理世界中的记忆展厅。每个场景都是对主人公回忆的深刻探索，从童年的回响到“家”的每一个角落。这些记忆片段，就像精美的画作，共同构成了“家”所代表的集体记忆。通过主人公的内心独白和象征性的视觉隐喻，这部影片不仅追溯了主人公家乡的历史，还深入探讨了记忆在我们大脑中的作用以及它们如何塑造我们的身份和对世界的理解。随着故事的展开，观众和主人公都会感受到，尽管时间的无情流逝，那些记忆所培养的情感将永远留在我们心中，成为我们不可分割的一部分。这部短片是对记忆、家庭和时间本质的视觉和情感探索。这部短片运用了大量的人工智能和 3D 扫描技术，例如 RUNWAY、稳定扩散、Blender、COLMAP、METASHAPE 和 Luma AI，探索了各种技术在叙事电影制作中的应用潜力。
desc_en: This short film is an introspective journey that takes place deep within the mind, inviting viewers into a gallery of memories within one’s psyche. Each scene is a profound exploration of the protagonist's recollections, from the echoes of childhood to every nook of 'home.' These memory fragments, like delicate paintings, come together to form a collective memory of what 'home' represents.Through the protagonist’s internal monologue and symbolic visual metaphors, the film not only traces the history of the protagonist's hometown but also delves into the function of memories within our brains and how they shape our identity and understanding of the world. As the story unfolds, the audience, along with the protagonist, will feel that despite the relentless march of time, the emotions cultivated by those memories will forever remain in our hearts as an inalienable part of us. This short film is a visual and emotional exploration of memory, family, and the essence of time.The short film employs a plethora of artificial intelligence and 3D scanning technologies, such as RUNWAY, Stable Diffusion, Blender, COLMAP, METASHAPE, and Luma AI, exploring the potential of various technologies in narrative filmmaking.
honor:
1.MIT AI for Filmmaking Hackathon 2024 Most Creative Use of AI

2.北京电影节 AIGC 2024 最佳视觉特效奖

Beijing Film Festival AIGC 2024 Best VFX Award
publication: The Immersive Art Therapy Driven by AIGC: An Innovative Approach to Alleviating Children's Nyctophobia
thanks: 冯宇纯、王志鹏、诸葛瑞静

title: 《虚境 现场》华晨宇火星演唱会艺术装置名称
desc: 2024年5月1日至4日，伴随着歌手华晨宇的火星演唱会，由华晨宇工作室、火星演唱会联合中国美术学院出品的装置艺术展“虚境：现场”在山东省烟台市养马岛音乐营地（牟平区里蹦岛）同步展出。
desc_en:
此次艺术展由中国美术学院创新设计学院团队的17名师生历经4个月创作完成。8件（组）综合媒介的大型互动装置作品，布置在华晨宇演唱会舞台的东南方位，占地幅面约5千平方米。装置群以从演唱会主舞台台口导出的中轴线为基准（见下图技术线稿），依据中心对称和有机落点的设计原则排布。作品运用聚散、放射、点阵、条线、模块等形式语言，结合镜面不锈钢、镭射气膜、频闪灯、LED灯带、烟雾机等材料与设备运用，在编码程控的调配下，从主舞台向休息区勾连出一片视听联觉的叙事结构体。同时，配合华晨宇演唱会日场和夜场不同的户外环境光线的变化，作品分时段体现折射、透射、反射和自发光等综合光效，让数万“火星人”歌迷得以在演唱会的音乐间歇里，充分体验光色流连、万物共生的超时空地景艺术现场。
 我参与了展览前期用户需求调研，草图提案，负责作品《神树》、《宝镜之城》的设计。
thanks: 有幸参与了我本科学校和我最喜欢的歌手合作，见证了华语音乐史上最伟大的日出。感谢指导老师顾文甲、陈炜博和团队的同学。

title: Project - 13
desc: 知汇通过策划 AIGC、人机交互与可编辑材料领域的系列讲座， 结合线上社群运营与知识管理，搭建高校艺术设计群体的学术交流平台，推动前沿技术与设计实践的融合 。讲座聚焦AIGC 艺术创作、智能交互设计和可编程材料等前沿领域，通过定期邀请学术界与产业界研究者进行专题分享，致力于打造一个持续更新的知识交流渠道。
thanks: 感谢中国美术学院校团委的大力支持

desc: 创新设计学院基础课程作业
